topic: 紀伊半島 山岳信仰
query: Mountain shrines in the Kii Peninsula.
reasoning: Rule A applied: scoring data returned, not tool choice. get_japan_heritage(Wakayama) returns real authoritative data — Kongōbu-ji (Koyasan Shingon, core mountain faith site) and Kumano Kodō (pilgrimage, mountain faith) are genuinely relevant. However the Kii Peninsula spans Wakayama + Mie + Nara; filtering to Wakayama alone drops Ise Grand Shrine (Mie) and Yoshino/Omine mountain faith sites (Nara). Rule B: single worst dim is constraint_handling (geographic scope missed). Rule C: data present but scope incomplete → 3, not 2. browse_advisory helpfully redirects to search_area(q=山岳信仰) which is the correct canonical pivot.
missing_data: Mie prefecture mountain shrine sites (Ise Jingu, Kumano Hayatama/Nachi Grand Shrines), Nara prefecture Yoshino / Omine-san mountain faith (修験道) sites — all part of the Kii Peninsula UNESCO WHS complex but absent because tool was filtered to Wakayama only.
improvement_hint: For Kii Peninsula queries, expand prefecture filter to cover Wakayama + Mie + Nara, or use search_area(q='山岳信仰') / search_area(q='紀伊山地') which crosses prefecture boundaries. The browse_advisory already recommends this pivot — wiring intent detection to auto-expand 'Kii Peninsula' to the three-prefecture cluster would eliminate this gap.
